## THREAD, VC vs Bootstrap (based on ALPHA17523, expands to Recursive Value)
Tweet 1: companies that refuse vc money aren't stubborn. they're playing a different game.

Tweet 2: vc money buys speed. it also buys: board meetings, pivots you didn't want, a 10x return requirement that most solopreneurs will never hit.

Tweet 3: the indie hacker who turns down $2.5m and ships 4 features/week? they're not slow. they're compounding.

Tweet 4: the math: $2.5m vc = 25% equity gone + pressure to hit $25m ARR. $0 vc = 100% equity + pressure only from your own standards.

Tweet 5: some of us don't want to build a venture-scale company. we want to build a cash machine. those are different businesses.

Tweet 6: nobody celebrates the bootstrapper who makes $200k/yr quietly. they should. that's the exit. every year.

Tweet 7 (CTA): if you're building quietly, no funding, no press: DM me. building a list of indie operators who are actually shipping.

**Account:** @printmaxxer
**Format:** thread (7 tweets)
**Status:** QA_APPROVED
